hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific TM5/ECL2 complementarity, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 binding by avoiding unfavorable interactions in conserved pockets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving good ddG but lack structural fidelity and docking quality. Tightening structural constraints and focusing on off-target disruption will improve both selectivity and design quality.

proposed_changes.rationale: Reducing the clash tolerance will enforce better structural quality and avoid steric clashes that lead to poor pLDDT and docking scores.

Focusing on non-pharmacophore positions known to contribute to off-target binding (SSTR3/SSTR5) will allow us to introduce mutations that disrupt off-target interactions while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14).
